APPLICATION PENDING FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY PETITIONER IN THE MATTER OF NANCY MARMOL STATE BAR NO. 87790 A MEMBER OF THE STATE BAR

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER IN THE MATTER OF NANCY MARMOL STATE BAR NO. 87790 NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUPERIOR COURT TO ASSUME JURISDICTION OVER LAW PRACTICE - MATTER TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 204 TO BE HEARD AT 9 A.M. BY JUDGE WISS. MR. BALIN TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION AND OR BRIEF NO LATER THAN AUGUST 24 WITH COURTESY COPIES TO BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 204 ADDRESSING THE FOLLOWING ISSUES 1) WHAT DOES MS. MARMOL MOVE FOR THE COURT TO DO, OTHER THAN ASSUME JURSIDCTION OVER HER PRACTICE? 2) DOES MS. MARMOL INVOKE PROBATE CODE 2468? 3) DOES MS. MARMOL MOVE FOR THE COURT TO APPOINT A PRACTICE ADMINISTRATOR AND, IF SO, WHO? 4) WHAT IS THE STATUS OF THE STATE BAR PROCEEDINGS THAT INVOLVE MS. MARMOL? 5) DOES MS. MARMOL HAVE LEGAL MALPRACTICE INSURANCE AND, IF SO, HAS HER MALPRACTICE CARRIER REFERRED OUT HER STATE BAR PROCEEDINGS FOR REPRESENTATION? JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUPERIOR COURT TO ASSUME JURISDICTION OVER LAW PRACTICE, CALENDAR OF SEP-07-2011 THE MATTER WAS REFERRED BACK TO THE LAW AND MOTION DEPARTMENT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE HAROLD KAHN MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING CRISTINA SAGANOWSKI. MS. SAGANOWSKI WAS REPRESENTED BY MS. MARMOL IN A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE THAT WAS BEFORE HIM WHICH WAS UNSUCCESFUL AND HER NAME APPEARS IN THE COMPLAINT WITH THE STATE BAR. THERE WERE NO OBJECTIONS. MATTER WAS DISCUSSED IN OPEN COURT. IN THE MATTER OF NANCY MARMOL STATE BAR NO. 87790 - PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR SUPERIOR COURT TO ASSUME JURISDICTION OVER LAW PRACTICE IS DENIED AND THE COURT THANKED MR. BALIN FOR HIS GOOD DEED. MR. BALIN TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Petitioner In The Matter Of Nancy Marmol State Bar No. 87790 Notice Of Motion For Superior Court To Assume Jurisdiction Over Law Practice
Off Calendar Oct-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM M. BALIN APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. CHRISTINE SOUHRADA, DEPUTY TRIAL COUNSEL, TEL. NO. 538-2183 APPEARING FOR THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING - JUDGE HAROLD KAHN MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING CRISTINA SAGANOWSKI. MS. SAGANOWSKI WAS REPRESENTED BY MS. MARMOL IN A SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE THAT WAS BEFORE HIM WHICH WAS UNSUCCESFUL AND HER NAME APPEARS IN THE COMPLAINT WITH THE STATE BAR. THERE WERE NO OBJECTIONS. MATTER WAS DISCUSSED IN OPEN COURT. In The Matter Of Nancy Marmol State Bar No. 87790 - Petitioner's Motion For Superior Court To Assume Jurisdiction Over Law Practice IS DENIED AND THE COURT THANK MR. BALIN FOR HIS GOOD DEED. MR. BALIN TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion For Superior Court To Assume Jurisdiction Over Law Practice
Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wonder Liang for State Bar. William Balin for Nancy Marmol.

Judge Wiss stated that the Probate Court does not have jurisdiction over this matter. The Court sent Counsel back to the Law and Motion Department. The matter is to be reset on October 7, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in the Law and Motion Department.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
